incorrects.description: Student selected 1: likely counting all items as one group or seeing the whole set as one

params.description: Both boxes highlight separately to show they are two distinct groups.

steps.text: Look closely. You see two separate boxes. Each box is a group. Count the boxes.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3: no clear pattern for this distractor in context

params.description: Both boxes highlight to emphasize there are exactly two groups.

steps.text: Here's a hint: count the boxes you see. Each box is one group.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5: likely counted items in one group instead of counting groups

params.description: Both boxes highlight as separate groups, not the items inside.

steps.text: You counted what's inside. We need to count the boxes themselves. How many boxes do you see?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System counts the boxes one at a time: box 1, box 2.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the boxes. One box. Two boxes. The answer is 2. We have 2 groups.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2: likely counted the groups instead of items in one group

params.description: One box highlights to focus attention on counting items inside it.

steps.text: You counted the boxes. Look inside one box. Count just the items in that one box.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3: no clear pattern for this distractor in context

params.description: One box highlights to focus counting on its contents.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. Pick one box and count the items inside it.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10: likely counted all items across both groups

params.description: One box highlights. Other box dims to focus on one group only.

steps.text: You counted everything. We need how many are in each group. Look at just one box.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights one box and counts items inside: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. I pick one box. I count the items inside: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. The answer is 5. Each group has 5 items.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 groups of 2: reversed the groups and items per group

params.description: Both boxes highlight separately, then one box's items highlight.

steps.text: You switched them. Count the boxes first. That tells you the groups. Then count inside one box. That tells you how many in each group.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System highlights the two boxes, then counts items in one box.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the boxes: 1, 2. That is 2 groups. I count inside one box: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. That is 5 in each group. The answer is 2 groups of 5. Groups come first, then how many in each.
